business.headline: Will your UPI payments be delayed by an hour? RBI proposal explainedThe Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released a discussion paper proposing a one-hour delay for certain digital payments. Here's an explainer on how it could work, which transactions it may affect, and what it means for users.

Sending over Rs 10,000 via UPI? RBI may soon introduce a 1-hour delayIf you often use UPI for bigger payments, a change may be on the way. The RBI is looking at adding a one-hour pause for transfers above Rs 10,000 to help prevent online fraud.

Applying for TAN? Income Tax Dept replaces Form 49B with two new formsApplying for a TAN is set to become a lot simpler. The Income Tax Department has replaced the old Form 49B with two new forms, aiming to make the process clearer and easier for different types of applicants. Let's have a look.

Infosys, TCS, HCLTech: Why IT stocks are down despite market rallyThe fall in IT stocks comes even as the Sensex and Nifty moved higher, showing that the weakness is limited to the sector amid concerns around growth and demand.

Sun Pharma shares fall over 4% amid acquisition concernsSun Pharma shares fell over 3.5%, as investor concerns over a potential mega acquisition outweighed optimism, bringing focus on debt, integration risks and the deal's long-term payoff.

TCS shares down 3%: Why the IT giant's stock fell despite strong Q4 result?TCS shares down: The stock was trading at around Rs 2,515.00, down 2.81% as of 10:15 am. It was also among the top losers on both the Nifty50 and the IT index.

Wipro shares rise as board considers buyback after three yearsWipro has informed exchanges that its board will evaluate a share buyback alongside its fourth-quarter results on April 16, bringing the focus back on shareholder returns.
Sensex gains 800 points: 3 reasons why stock market is rising todayThe S&P BSE Sensex climbed 687.99 points to 77,319.64, while the NSE Nifty50 added 211.60 points to 23,986.70 as of 9:58 am.
Ola Electric shares continue to rise, up 6% today. Here's whyAt 9:44 am, Ola Electric shares were trading at Rs 38.71, up Rs 2.37 or 6.52%. Yesterday, the stock closed nearly 20% higher.

Tata Sons IPO push gathers pace as trustees back listingA growing chorus within Tata Trusts is backing an IPO for Tata Sons, setting the stage for a high-stakes decision amid regulatory uncertainty, rising capital needs and a widening divide at the top.

Markets rally over improved global cues driven by financials; IT stocks downThe S&P BSE Sensex climbed 442.82 points to 77,074.48, while the NSE Nifty50 added 144.95 points to 23,920.05 as of 9:28 am.

TCS posts solid Q4, but growth concerns remain. Should you buy?TCS reported a consolidated net profit of Rs 13,718 crore for Q4 FY26, marking a sharp rise of nearly 29% from the previous quarter. Revenue from operations increased 5.4% quarter-on-quarter to Rs 70,698 crore.
Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market rise or fall today?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,909.50 as of 8:08 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 may open above Thursday's close of 23,775.10.
TCS Q4 results: 5 key takeaways from a steady quarterThe IT major reported a net profit of Rs 13,718 crore for Q4FY26, up 12.2% year-on-year, while revenue came in at Rs 70,698 crore.

Looking to invest in property in 2026? Here's how to spot the next big locationProperty prices don't rise overnight. They move quietly at first, often in places most people overlook. If you're planning to invest in 2026, the real question is — can you spot the shift before everyone else does?

TCS Q4 results: Profit rises 12% YoY, Rs 31 dividend announcedThe IT major posted a net profit of Rs 13,718 crore for Q4FY26, up 12.2% year-on-year, while revenue rose to Rs 70,698 crore. Sequentially, profit growth remained modest at around 2%, indicating a gradual recovery rather than a sharp rebound in demand.

Ola Electric rallies 20% as sales recover: Should you buy at current levels?The stock is now up around 25% in April, marking a sharp reversal after six straight months of losses. The trigger has been a turnaround in March sales, which has helped shift sentiment.

Sensex sinks over 900 points as shaky Iran-US ceasefire drags marketsThe sharp fall comes just a day after Dalal Street logged a strong rally, with the Sensex closing at 76,631.65, down 931.25 points or 1.20%, while the Nifty slipped 222.25 points, or 0.93%, to 23,775.10.

Personal loan on your mind? 7 simple checks to avoid costly mistakesA personal loan can feel like an easy solution when you need quick money. But what looks simple at first can turn expensive if you miss a few key details. A quick check now can save you stress later.

Crude oil prices drop, but is the damage already done?Crude oil has eased after the spike, but disrupted flows, vessel backlogs and persistent risks are already feeding into inflation and growth, with markets still adjusting to the shock.

entertainment.headline: Dacoit X review: Praise for Adivi Sesh and Mrunal Thakur, but film divides fans Adivi Sesh and Mrunal Thakur's Dacoit opened to mixed reviews, with praise for performances and music, but criticism over predictability and technical issues. The film released today.

Jana Nayagan leaked online but can Vijay's star power outrun piracy? Vijay's Jana Nayagan faces an unexpected crisis after a pre-release leak, raising serious questions about its box office future and audience anticipation. As delays mount and stakes rise, can the film reclaim its momentum, or has the damage already been done?

Salman Khan wishes Anant Ambani on birthday: He will lift the entire nation Salman Khan celebrated Anant Ambani's birthday by sharing a heartfelt message and a playful picture on social media. Salman Khan praised Anant's qualities in the photo caption.

Dan Levy tears up, says no Schitt's Creek sequel without Catherine O'Hara Actor Dan Levy emotionally ruled out a sequel to Schitt's Creek following the death of key cast member Catherine O'Hara. Fans across social media echo the sentiment, expressing that the beloved show should remain untouched as a tribute to her legacy.

Nani calls Adivi Sesh's Dacoit his best till date: True blockbuster with all heart Telugu actor Nani has hailed Adivi Sesh's performance in the romantic thriller Dacoit as his best work yet. Released worldwide on April 10, 2026, the movie is generating excitement among audiences for its gripping story and strong emotional narrative.

Ram Gopal Varma celebrates birthday with Dhurandhar twist, Kangana Ranaut reacts Ram Gopal Varma grabbed attention with a quirky Dhurandhar-inspired birthday post. His celebration in Mumbai was attended by several celebrities.

Dhurandhar star Ranveer Singh clipped belly to feel pain in Lootera: Anurag Kashyap Anurag Kashyap reveals Ranveer Singh's extreme dedication, recalling a shocking Lootera incident amid Dhurandhar 2 success.

Deepika Padukone paid nearly 7 times less than Allu Arjun for Raaka Allu Arjun and Deepika Padukone's Raaka is already making headlines ahead of release, not just for its scale but also for reported fees of its star cast.

Game of Thrones actor Michael Patrick dies at 35 after prolonged illness Michael Patrick, who had a brief role in Game of Thrones, died at 35 following a battle with motor neurone disease. His wife, Naomi Sheehan, paid an emotional tribute, honouring his life and spirit.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 22: Enters 4th weekend in style, Rs 1665 crore worldwide Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge continues its dominant box office run, inching closer to a major global milestone as it enters its fourth weekend. With strong weekday holds and no immediate competition, the film is now eyeing a new spot among the biggest Indian hits of all time.

Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce confirm wedding date, venue. Check out details Looks like it's finally happening – Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have locked the date for their big day, brushing aside earlier rumours. And if that wasn't enough, the duo is planning a globe-trotting, ultra-private honeymoon to kick off their next chapter in style.

Small films, big wins: 7 South Indian hits that surprised in 2026 so far Small budgets, big impact—these 7 films from 2026 so far prove content is king, turning minimal hype into massive box office success across industries through strong word of mouth and audience connect.

Vijay's Jana Nayagan, stuck in censor board limbo, leaked in HD on pirate sites As Vijay's Jana Nayagan continues to await its censor certificate, a high-definition (HD) print of the film has reportedly been leaked on pirated websites, raising alarm among filmmakers and fans. Authorities are now being urged to step in and curb the spread of piracy.

Dhurandhar's unseen hero: How Shashwat Sachdev's sound drives franchise's impact Shashwat Sachdev powers Dhurandhar's music with a soundtrack that blends emotion, energy, and storytelling. By mixing nostalgia with modern sounds and global voices, he creates songs that connect across generations and stay long after the film ends.

No remorse: Kunal Kamra refuses to apologise before panel for Eknath Shinde joke The controversy dates back to a performance last year in which Kunal Kamra referred to Eknath Shinde as a "traitor" over his 2022 rebellion that split the Shiv Sena led by Uddhav Thackeray.

Dhurandhar 2 song row: Delhi High Court appoints mediator in Oye oye copyright case The Delhi High Court has referred the copyright dispute over the song Rang De Lal from Dhurandhar 2 to mediation, aiming to resolve financial and licencing issues between Trimurti Films and B62 Studios.

Still Alive works for Samay Raina, but Apoorva Mukhija makes it matter Samay Raina's Still Alive may be his comeback story, but its most revealing moment lies in his retelling of the Apoorva Mukhija episode, where a viral clash exposes the gendered cost of speaking back.

Aditya Dhar praises Sandeep Vanga's stand on Dhurandhar 2, commends his honesty Filmmaker Aditya Dhar appreciated Sandeep Reddy Vanga's support for Dhurandhar 2. He also noted that he looks up to the Spirit director for his honest take on cinema.

Vishal Bhardwaj allegedly likes post comparing Dhurandhar fans to Nazis, faces flak Filmmaker Vishal Bhardwaj has sparked a social media storm after allegedly liking a reel that controversially compared Dhurandhar fans to Nazis. The incident has ignited mixed reactions online.

general news.headline: Rs 3,000 for women, Uniform Civil Code in 6 months if BJP wins Bengal: Amit ShahUnveiling the BJP's West Bengal manifesto, Home Minister Amit Shah termed Mamata Banerjee's 15-year rule a "period of darkness", alleging widespread fear among people and a growing demand for change.

Justice Yashwant Varma resigns a year after huge cash recovery at homeA resignation amid controversy raises fresh questions over accountability in the judiciary. What led to Justice Varma's exit, and what could follow next?

Why Pakistan scrambled fighter jets to Middle East ahead of US-Iran talksAll eyes will be on Islamabad this weekend as it hosts the most consequential diplomatic meeting in recent years - talks between the US and Iran to end the war. Pakistan has left no stone unturned, deploying an air armada to the Middle East. Here's why.

Trinamool's sting of Humayun Kabir goes viral, AIMIM calls off allianceThe video purportedly shows Humayun Kabir claiming links with BJP leaders and discussing a plan to divert minority votes in Bengal in return for large funds, including an alleged advance payment of Rs 200 crore.

Genghis Khan was not a Muslim. In fact, he massacred themThe Iranian Embassy in South Africa likened Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu to Genghis Khan, invoking him to condemn the destruction caused by the war on Iran. It was Mongol leader Genghis Khan and his armies, who ran over and destroyed much of the early Islamic kingdoms, massacring millions of Muslims across Iran and Central Asia.

Traffic curbs, schools, markets shut: Islamabad turns fortress for US-Iran talksThe upcoming negotiations in Islamabad come in the wake of a two-week ceasefire between the US & Iran, announced after a period of intensifying conflict triggered by the February 28 killing of Iran's Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

Laughter turns to screams as 3 girls drown taking selfies at Andhra waterfallAccording to police, the girls were standing on slippery rocks close to the falls and trying to take selfies when they lost their footing and slipped into the water. Officials said none of them knew how to swim.

Pak's Khawaja Asif deletes Israel is evil post after Netanyahu's fiery responseA sharp war of words erupts as Israel questions Pakistan's neutrality in US-Iran talks after explosive remarks by its defence minister. Can Islamabad still hold its ground as a peace broker?

Why US-Iran talks in Pakistan are built to failAs US and Iranian officials prepare to meet in Pakistan on April 11, the talks come amid intensifying Israeli strikes in Lebanon, which Tehran sees as a direct violation of a key ceasefire condition. With Israel, the central actor driving the Lebanon front, absent from the negotiating table, a critical question hangs over the process: can these talks deliver any meaningful outcome?

Amity, Ashoka join IITs in shaping the Cognitive Science revolution, driving Artificial IntelligenceCognitive science is, by its very nature, a field that requires the simultaneous presence of psychology, neuroscience, computer science, linguistics and philosophy.

Will your UPI payments be delayed by an hour? RBI proposal explainedThe Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released a discussion paper proposing a one-hour delay for certain digital payments. Here's an explainer on how it could work, which transactions it may affect, and what it means for users.

Army clears Col Purohit for Brigadier rank after 17-year legal battleFrom arrest to acquittal and now promotion, Col Purohit's long legal battle takes a decisive turn. But how did a stalled career move back on track after years in limbo?

Beyond the jokes: Samay Raina and the reality of psychosisWhile in the US, Samay Raina experienced a psychosis episode after finding out about the FIRs against him. His decision to open up about the experience has started a wider conversation about the condition.

Hate crimes against Sikhs surged by 3,700% in US in last 10 years: ReportA decade-long surge in hate crimes against Sikhs in the US raises alarm, even as overall incidents dip. What explains this sharp rise, and which other communities are seeing similar trends?

You're dark-skinned, I deserve better: Woman kills husband with lover's helpDuring the probe, the victim's family said the marriage was troubled and marked by frequent arguments, alleging the accused women, Priyanka, often taunted her husband, including over his appearance.

Live: One killed in strikes on Lebanon as Israel continues blitz amid ceasefireUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: US President Donald Trump's push to steady the Middle East crisis is colliding with hard realities on the ground, as calls for restraint and negotiations struggle to match the pace of military action. The US has quietly urged Israel to tone down its campaign and move towards dialogue, with Trump emphasising a more "low-key" approach even as signals from Tel Aviv remain mixed. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has opened the door to talks, but continues to maintain that operations against Hezbollah will not pause. The fallout has been severe, with more than 300 people killed in Lebanon. At the same time, Beirut has begun indicating a shift in its own stance, with moves targeting armed groups seen as part of a broader attempt to align with diplomatic efforts. Iran has responded with sharp warnings, arguing that ongoing attacks undermine the very basis of negotiations and risk collapsing an already fragile truce. In a defiant statement, Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei said on Thursday that Iran would be "resolute in avenging" the deaths of his father Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and the country's "martyrs" and "will take management of the Strait of Hormuz into a new phase." Meanwhile, the United States is preparing for crucial talks in Pakistan, which will be held on April 11. Catch all the live updates at Indiatoday.in.

What's behind Melania's sudden Epstein announcement?When the White House said Melania Trump would make an announcement, nobody knew what it was about. A statement on America's biggest sex trafficker was the last thing anyone could think of.

Trump says oil will flow quickly, with or without Iran amid Hormuz tensionsTrump says oil will flow with or without Iran, warns Tehran against tanker fees in Hormuz, claims victory, and attacks critics, offering no details as tensions around the vital route persist.

Live: Amit Shah's 'detect, delete, deport' promise on infiltrators in BengalAssembly Election News 2026 Live Updates: Campaigning and polling activity gathered pace across states in the Assembly Election 2026 cycle. Vijay began outreach in Madurai to expand his party's grassroots presence in Tamil Nadu. Voting in Assam, Kerala and Puducherry concluded peacefully with record turnouts, supported by full webcasting and voter-friendly measures. Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar called it a "historic" democratic exercise. Meanwhile, in West Bengal, Amit Shah released the BJP's 'Sankalp Patra' in Kolkata, alongside rallies and roadshows to boost campaign momentum. Stay with IndiaToday.in for latest and live updates-

Authorities are now being urged to step in and curb the spread of piracy.

Tiny Amur falcons begin epic 5,000 km return journey from Africa to IndiaApapang, Alang, and Ahu, the three Amur Falcons that began their migratory journey from India, have completed their trip and are now heading back to India. Read about their journey and what's ahead.

Aditya Dhar praises Sandeep Vanga's stand on Dhurandhar 2, commends his honestyFilmmaker Aditya Dhar appreciated Sandeep Reddy Vanga's support for Dhurandhar 2. He also noted that he looks up to the Spirit director for his honest take on cinema.

Vietnam reacts to India Today report on Indians being ill-treatedVietnam, in its response to an India Today Digital report, assured action after several Indian tourists reported discrimination during their visit. The tourists complained their experiences have been marred by uncomfortable and, at times, hostile interactions with locals.

sports.headline: Kohli, Sooryavanshi headline battle of generations in Guwahati IPL 2026: Two unbeaten sides in the Indian Premier League, Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Rajasthan Royals, are set to face each other on Friday, April 10. Can the underdog Royals present a challenge to the high-flying RCB?

Badminton Asia: Shetty stuns 3rd seed to reach semis, keeps India's hope alive India's medal hope in the Badminton Asia Championships remained alive as Ayush Shetty stunned third seed Jonatan Christie to reach the semi-finals of the competition. Shetty beat the Indonesian in straight games, in a match that lasted nearly one hour.

Is Vaibhav Sooryavanshi destined for Rohit Sharma-Virat Kohli stardom? IPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's explosive start to the season has not just grabbed attention, it has sparked talk of something much bigger. With Graeme Smith backing his rise, the 15-year-old is already being seen as a future superstar in the making.

Sanjiv Goenka embraces Mukul Chaudhary after LSG hero's power knock vs KKR IPL 2026: Mukul Choudhary's stunning finish against KKR did not just win LSG the game, it earned him a heartfelt embrace from owner Sanjiv Goenka. The viral moment captured both the emotion of the win and the arrival of a new match-winner.

FIFA scams fans: World Cup ticket buyers accuse body of cheating FIFA is facing backlash from fans after quietly introducing higher-priced "front category" World Cup tickets. Many hit out against FIFA on social media, alleging that the body shifted the previously purchased seats to less favourable locations.

KKR unhappy with umpiring after Digvesh Rathi's controversial catch IPL 2026: KKR were left questioning umpiring standards after a controversial catch decision saw Finn Allen dismissed against LSG. While Rovman Powell called it a blunder, the incident has once again put consistency under the spotlight.

How one advice from Rishabh Pant unlocked Mukul Choudhary's best vs KKR IPL 2026: Mukul Choudhary's match-winning finish against KKR came down to one thing, clearing his mind and trusting his game after a timely word from Rishabh Pant. In a chase that looked done at 128/7, that small reset made all the difference.

IPL 2026 Play Of The Day: Mukul summons Dhoni's helicopter to blow KKR away IPL 2026: On a thrilling night at the Eden, the helicopter shot once again made its reappearance, this time with a new pilot at the helm in Mukul Choudhary. A fan of MS Dhoni, Mukul channeled his idol perfectly to pull off a heist against KKR.

Runs like Virat, chases like Dhoni: How LSG discovered Mukul Choudhary After Mukul Choudhary's epic finishing act stunned KKR, Lucknow Super Giants head coach Justin Langer revealed that it was performance analyst Shrinivas Chandrasekaran who first flagged the young Rajasthan wicketkeeper to the team management ahead of the auction.

Candidates chess: Vaishali jumps to sole lead among women, Praggnanandhaa loses R Vaishali stayed in strong form in the women's Candidates, moving into sole lead after drawing with Anna Muzychuk in Round 10, while Javokhir Sindarov strengthened his position at the top of the open section with a win over R Praggnanandhaa on Thursday.

Living his father's dream, Jhunjhunu's Mukul fights like a jawan vs KKR Jhunjhunu's Mukul Choudhary channels a soldier's grit to lead Lucknow Super Giants to a memorable last-ball thrilling win over Kolkata Knight Riders at the Eden Gardens on Thursday, April 9.

Heartbreak at Eden: Mukul Choudhary's epic heist seals KKR's fate in KolkataKKR vs LSG: An epic rescue act from Mukul Choudhary helped Lucknow Super Giants snatch victory from Kolkata Knight Riders on Thursday. KKR lost their third game of the season, and remain winless in IPL 2026.

Who is Mukul Choudhary? 21-year-old MS Dhoni fan stuns KKR with helicopter shotsAn MS Dhoni admirer, Mukul Choudhary produced the knock of his career to guide Lucknow Super Giants to a three-wicket win over Kolkata Knight Riders in their IPL 2026 clash at Eden Gardens on Thursday, finishing off the chase with a composed display under pressure.

Slammed for cheating KKR, Cameron Green strikes in his first over in IPL 2026IPL 2026: Slammed for not bowling for KKR despite a hefty price tag, Cameron Green struck on his very first over for the franchise in IPL 2026. Green's bouncer got the better of LSG captain Rishabh Pant.

Asian Boxing Championships: Preeti Pawar, Minakshi Hooda lead India's gold rushReigning world champion Minakshi Hooda and Preeti Pawar led India's gold rush, as two other compatriots also won their respective finals to be crowned Asian boxing champions on Thursday.

Ravi Shastri stand unveiled at Wankhede; Edulji, Solkar, Sardesai too honouredA stand named after former India all-rounder Ravi Shastri was unveiled at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai on Thursday. The Mumbai Cricket Association (MCA) also honoured other cricketing greats, including Diana Edulji, Eknath Solkar and Dilip Sardesai during the ceremony.

Will India miss FIFA World Cup? Broadcast rights unsold 2 months before eventIndian football fans face uncertainty as FIFA World Cup 2026 media rights remain unsold just two months before the tournament, raising doubts over live broadcast in India. Here's the reasons why there is no interest in getting the media rights for the tournament.

Finn Allen given out despite Digvesh Rathi touching rope during catchYet another umpiring howler took place in the Indian Premier League this season during KKR's match against LSG. The incident occurred in the 2nd over of the match, where Finn Allen was given caught out, despite Digvesh Rathi touching the rope while catching the ball.

Fiesty Rahane snaps back at toss, updates on Cameron Green's bowling fitnessKKR captain Ajinkya Rahane snapped back at the toss in their match against Lucknow Super Giants on Thursday, April 9. Speaking to Pommie Mbangwa, Rahane defended his toss call, which had put him on the spotlight in the Punjab Kings game.

CD Gopinath, member of India's first-ever Test-winning team, dies at 96India's oldest living Test cricketer, C. D. Gopinath, passed away at the age of 96 on Thursday. He was part of the Indian team that defeated England for their first-ever Test victory in 1952.

IPL 2026: Ahead of RR vs RCB clash, Riyan Parag casts vote during Assam pollsRajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag fulfilled his responsibility as a citizen first before the clash against the Royal Challengers Bengaluru in Guwahati on Thursday, April 9. Riyan cast his vote alongside his family before the game.

Shreyas Iyer's sister faces online abuse, deletes video from IPL matchShreyas Iyer's sister, Shresta, faced online abuse for a video she posted on Instagram after the KKR vs PBKS match. She deleted that Instagram video after facing online abuse, saying it was meant as light-hearted banter but was misunderstood by many.

Dhoni my wicketkeeping inspiration, Impact rule good for youngsters: PrabhsimranPunjab Kings power-hitter Prabhsimran Singh has expressed strong support for the 'Impact Sub' rule in the Indian Premier League, describing it as a valuable lifeline for young players and an opportunity to showcase their talent.

3 overs, 9 wickets: Brazil women's cricketer Laura Cardoso creates T20I historyBrazil Women all-rounder Laura Cardoso becomes the first player in men's or women's T20I history to take nine wickets in an innings, finishing with 9/4 vs Lesotho. Brazil won the match by 189 runs.

Saudi League is rigged for Ronaldo: Fans call out horrible refereeingSaudi Pro League was at the centre of the controversy after Al Ahli hit out against the poor standards of the league. Fans, experts and even footballers joined in the criticism, with some accusing the league of favouring Cristiano Ronaldo's Al Nassr.

IPL 2026: Delhi cricket director alleges ticket fraud during DC vs MI clashDDCA director Anand Verma alleges ticket fraud during the Delhi Capitals vs Mumbai Indians IPL 2026 match at Arun Jaitley Stadium after valid ticket holders were denied entry over duplicate scans.

KKR vs LSG Highlights: Mukul Choudhary gets LSG over the line in thrillerKolkata Knight Riders (KKR) vs Lucknow Super Giants (LSG), IPL 2026 Highlights: Mukul Choudhary steals the show at the Eden Gardens and pulls the win for LSG as KKR are defeated once again. The death bowling of the home side wasn't up to the mark and they allowed Mukul to get Lucknow over the line.

3-6 months to live: Yuvraj shares what doctor told him after cancer diagnosisYuvraj Singh has opened up on his battle with cancer, revealing how he ignored symptoms while playing for India before a diagnosis forced him into treatment and a long road to recovery.

Inspired by Harmanpreet Kaur, Niki Prasad weaves her own World Cup dreamIn an interview with India Today, Niki Prasad spoke about her ambition to help India win a World Cup, following in the footsteps of Harmanpreet Kaur, who achieved the milestone last year by winning the Women's ODI World Cup.

HS Prannoy slams Badminton Asia Champs over unfair treatment, slams organisersAyush Shetty continued his impressive run at the Badminton Asia Championships 2026, defeating Chi Yu Jen to reach the men's singles quarterfinals in Ningbo. Meanwhile, HS Prannoy and PV Sindhu were both knocked out in the Round of 16, ending their campaigns.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
